article_title: Microsoft's April 2026 Patch Tuesday Ships 163 Fixes, Including an Exploited SharePoint Spoofing Flaw and a Publicly Disclosed Defender Escalation

content_preview.title: Microsoft's April 2026 Patch Tuesday Ships 163 Fixes, Including an Exploited SharePoint Spoofing Flaw and a Publicly Disclosed Defender Escalation
content_preview.summary: April's update is Microsoft's second-largest Patch Tuesday on record, with 8 critical flaws, two zero-days, and privilege escalation bugs accounting for well over half of the patches.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Microsoft released its April 2026 Patch Tuesday updates on April 14, addressing 163 vulnerabilities across Windows, Office, SharePoint, Active Directory, Defender, and networking components. According to [Tenable](https://www.tenable.com/blog/microsofts-april-2026-patch-tuesday-addresses-163-cves-cve-2026-32201), it is the second-largest monthly release in Microsoft's history, trailing only the record set earlier in the year. Two of the patched flaws qualify as zero-days: one active...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong infosec article on Microsoft April 2026 Patch Tuesday.
editor_notes.source_verification: Tenable VERIFIED via WebFetch: 163 CVEs (8 critical, 154 important, 1 moderate), CVE-2026-32201 SharePoint spoofing exploited zero-day, CVE-2026-33825 Defender EoP 'BlueHammer' publicly disclosed, CVE-2026-33824 IKE RCE CVSS 9.8, CVE-2026-33826 AD RCE CVSS 8.0, second-largest PT all confirmed.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All CVE IDs, CVSS scores, severity counts verified.
editor_notes.originality: No prior April Patch Tuesday coverage.
